name: Sidney Poitier
description: Bahamian and American actor and diplomat (1927–2022)
abstract: Sidney Poitier ( PWAH-tyay; February 20, 1927 – January 6, 2022) was a Bahamian-American actor, film director, activist, and diplomat. In 1964, he was the first black actor and first Bahamian to win the Academy Award for Best Actor.

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: How did "For Love of Ivy" come about? That was with Sidney Poitier. ... Everybody thought Diahann Carroll or Barbara McNair or anybody but Abbey Lincoln was going to make this film. ... it was mine, and it was very successful.
about_quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris
about_quotes.evidence: He wasn’t handsome by standards of handsomness, like he wasn’t a Harry Belafonte or a Sidney Poitier.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 21, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] That was the Duke Ellington with Sidney Poitier and Diahann Carroll in ’61. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, and that’s when the little hanky-panky was going?
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: they can get me cheaper than them other actors such as Harry Belafonte, Sidney Poitier and Sammy Davis, Jr., and Lena Horne.

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: was kind of like a thrill for me to be there on that set, with Sidney Poitier, Harry Belafonte and-all the names.

about_quotes.speaker: Horn Shirley
about_quotes.evidence: Sidney Poitier came and I got weak in the knees, and he said, ―Miss Horn, I really enjoyed your music.‖
about_quotes.doc_title: Horn Shirley — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
